content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660              322-5901            322-6441              322-6444

October 7, 1983

Mickey R. Conroy, President
Armed Forces Retirees
Association, California
P.O. Box 10357
Santa Ana, CA  92711

Re:  Advice Letter No. A-83-207

Dear Mr. Conroy:

     Thank you for your letter of August 31, 1983.  The Fair
Political Practices Commission only enforces the Political
Reform Act.  That Act does not prohibit expenditures of state
funds or use of state employees or facilities for political
campaigns.  It does, however, require reporting of such
expenditures in certain situations.  See 2 Cal. Adm. Code
Section 18420 (copy enclosed).  As the comment to the regulation
points out, however, such expenditures may be illegal.

     If you believe that public funds or resources have been used
for campaign purposes without being properly reported under the
Political Reform Act, you may contact either the Enforcement
Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission or your
local district attorney.  If you believe that state resources
have been illegally used for campaign purposes, you may contact
either the State Attorney General or the district attorney.

Very truly yours,

Barbara A. Milman
Chief
Legal Division

BAM:plh
Enclosure

State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statement of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660               322-5901             322-6441          372-6444

September 12, 1983


Mickey R. Conroy
President
Armed Forces Retirees
Association, California
P.O. Box 10357
Santa Ana, CA 92711
                                                    Re: A-83-207

Dear Mr. Conroy:

     Your letter requesting advice under the Political
Reform Act has been referred to me. If you have any
questions about your advice request, please contact me
directly at (916) 322-5901.

     We try to answer all advice requests promptly.
Therefore, unless your request poses particularly complex
legal questions, or unless more information is needed to
answer your request, you should expect a response within 21
working days.

                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Barbara A. Milman
                                        Chief
                                        Legal Division

BAM:km
Enclosure

Armed Forces Retirees Association,
California
Post Office Box 10357
Santa Ana, California 92711

August 31, 1983

Chairman
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 Kay Street
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804-0807

Dear Sir:

I would like to obtain your opinion and comments on the
following questions:

1. Is there any violation in law when a member
of the Legislature reproduces and distributes
campaign literature at State expense?

2. Is it against the law to use State employees'
time and State facilities to do the same?

I would appreciate a response.

Sincerely,

Mickey R. Conroy
President

MRC/ac

sections.question: 1. Is there any violation in law when a member of the Legislature reproduces and distributes campaign literature at state expense? 2. Is it against the law to use State employees' time and State facilities to do the same?
sections.conclusion: The Fair Political Practices Commission only enforces the Political Reform Act. That Act does not prohibit expenditures of state funds or use of state employees or facilities for political campaigns. It does, however, require reporting of such expenditures in certain situations. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18420 (copy enclosed). As the comment to the regulation points out, however, such expenditures may be illegal. If you believe that public funds or resources have been used for campaign purposes without being properly reported under the Political Reform Act, you may contact either the Enforcement Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission or your local district attorney. If you believe that state resources have been illegally used for campaign purposes, you may contact either the State Attorney General or the district attorney.
sections.question_synthetic: The requester asks whether it is unlawful for a state legislator to reproduce and distribute campaign literature using state funds, employees' time, and state facilities.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The Political Reform Act does not itself prohibit use of state funds or resources for campaigns but requires reporting such expenditures under certain circumstances. Such expenditures may, however, be illegal under other laws, and the requester should contact the FPPC Enforcement Division, Attorney General, or district attorney to report suspected illegal use of state resources.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both incoming request letter (dated August 31, 1983) and FPPC response letter (dated October 7, 1983). Question and conclusion sections are present in their respective letters. Response signed by Barbara A. Milman, Chief, Legal Division.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: 1. Is there any violation in law when a member of the Legislature reproduces and distributes campaign literature at state expense? 2. Is it against the law to use State employees' time and State facilities to do the same?

CONCLUSION: The Fair Political Practices Commission only enforces the Political Reform Act. That Act does not prohibit expenditures of state funds or use of state employees or facilities for political campaigns. It does, however, require reporting of such expenditures in certain situations. See 2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18420 (copy enclosed). As the comment to the regulation points out, however, such expenditures may be illegal. If you believe that public funds or resources have been used for campaign purposes without being properly reported under the Political Reform Act, you may contact either the Enforcement Division of the Fair Political Practices Commission or your local district attorney. If you believe that state resources have been illegally used for campaign purposes, you may contact either the State Attorney General or the district attorney.

embedding.summary: Mickey R. Conroy of the Armed Forces Retirees Association inquired whether state legislators may lawfully use state funds, employees, and facilities to reproduce and distribute campaign literature. The FPPC responded that while the Political Reform Act does not prohibit such use, it requires reporting and such conduct may violate other laws.
